arguments.task: Visit the official DealCloud API documentation and related first-party pages. Determine: 1) what API surface types are offered (REST, GraphQL, both, or none), 2) documented authentication methods, 3) any rate limit notes, 4) whether access appears self-serve or gated, including any references to developer account signup, partner/contact-sales, or customer-only access, and 5) any first-party links to changelog, GitHub, MCP server/registry, or support docs. Prefer official documentation pages on api.docs.dealcloud.com and related DealCloud/Intapp domains. Return concise findings with exact source URLs for each finding.

input.thought: Start browser-based extraction from the official DealCloud API docs and related first-party pages.

arguments.task: Using only official DealCloud or Intapp pages, extract exact evidence with URLs for these items: 1) API authentication methods and any required permissions or customer account requirements from the API Keys and OAuth docs; 2) rate limit values from the limits page; 3) whether the API is REST or GraphQL from the docs home or API reference; 4) whether an MCP server exists from the MCP page; 5) a first-party release notes or changelog URL; and 6) whether there is any public self-serve signup or instead customer/admin gating. Return a concise bullet list with exact quoted facts and source URLs.

raw_result.description: CRM platform for deal, relationship, and pipeline management with a documented DealCloud API for customer data and workflow integrations.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official docs include an API Keys guide and a token-based auth guide; evidence gathered indicates API key generation requires an existing DealCloud account plus specific user-group/API capability permissions. The exact token standard and whether OAuth2 is formally supported were not fully verified from final extracted text, so token auth is marked with human review.
raw_result.gating_reason: API access appears customer/admin gated rather than public self-serve: gathered evidence indicates users need an existing DealCloud account and permission to generate API keys via an API capability/user-group setting.

api_surface.breadth: Public docs show a broad documented API with guides, API references, limits, SDK sections, release notes, and product areas including authentication, user management, data, schema, RelIntelligence, publications, and eMarketing.
api_surface.mcp_notes: The official docs include an /mcp section and gathered evidence noted an MCP server/client preview, but implementation maturity and public availability details remain unclear.

raw_result.blocker: Public documentation exists, but initial toolkit build likely requires being an existing DealCloud customer and obtaining admin-enabled API access/credentials; some auth and MCP details remain ambiguous from the gathered evidence.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Browser-gathered evidence from the official limits page indicates documented per-area limits, with notes mentioning values such as 100 requests/minute, 2,000 requests/hour, and 10,000 requests/day; exact endpoint-by-endpoint mapping should be rechecked manually on the limits table.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official API docs are publicly accessible; docs include SDK pages (including C# and Python sections), release notes/changelog, explicit limits documentation, and an MCP page, all of which are positive signals for agent/toolkit feasibility.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Need manual verification of the exact token auth type (token vs OAuth2), the precise rate-limit table values by API area, whether MCP is publicly usable today vs preview, and whether access tier should be classified as paid-plan-required versus admin-approval-required based on customer entitlement details.
